extraction.one_line_summary: KRAGH ANDERSEN Søren won his second stage after covering 31.6 miles in the opening hour, the highest average of the Tour, with MEZGEC second for the second time to him.
tactical_patterns: CAVAGNA Rémi's early shredding attack at 73 miles out set record Tour pace, draining chaser leadout men while preserving Quick-Step's BENNETT support.
KRAGH ANDERSEN won both sprint stages where he personally drove opening-hour pace above Tour average, suggesting deliberate solo fatigue strategy.
Quick-Step placed both VAN BAARLE Dylan and STUYVEN Jasper in break alongside CAVAGNA, triple-loading to neutralize any organized chase from rivals.
BENNETT Sam deliberately avoided stage victory sprint despite green jersey competition, prioritizing survival over marginal points accumulation with 50-point buffer.

rider_signals.KRAGH ANDERSEN Søren: positive: Second stage win with identical pattern—record opening hour pace followed by sprint victory suggests repeatable tactical dominance.
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: positive: Drove breakaway at 31.6mph opening hour, highest Tour average, demonstrating exceptional one-day sacrifice capacity for team tactics.
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: neutral: Strategically avoided stage win sprint despite position, showing calculated risk management with green jersey nearly secured.
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: Lost leadout man PÖSTLBERGER to bee sting retirement stage 19, tactical disadvantage compounding points deficit.
rider_signals.MEZGEC Luka: neutral: Second consecutive runner-up to KRAGH ANDERSEN in identical sprint scenarios suggests ceiling in stage-hunting ability.
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: Holds two-point polka dot lead into stage 20 uphill time trial, creating strategic dilemma between classification defense and conservation.
